commonProblems.h2: Mercedes Benz Citan Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.group: Mercedes Citan OM607 Diesel Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Citan OM607 Diesel Timing Chain Failure - Why Tensioner Wear Leads to Catastrophic Engine Damage

repairOptions.tier: Preventative - Timing Chain Replacement (before failure symptoms)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full timing chain kit replacement: chain, tensioner, guides, sprockets. Crank pulley and timing cover reseal.
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles. Recommended for high-mileage OM607s as preventative maintenance.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine (after chain failure)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt OM607 engine with upgraded timing components, new EGR cooler, full gasket set, bench tested.
repairOptions.longevity: Engine returned to factory spec with upgraded components addressing original failure points.

problems.recommendation: If you catch the chain rattle early on cold starts - before any running issues or warning lights - a preventative timing chain replacement at an independent specialist makes financial sense on a Citan valued at £2,000-£4,000. However, if the engine has already jumped timing or snapped the chain, replacement is almost always the better value than attempting a full rebuild of a damaged engine. Independent specialists typically charge 30-40% less than main dealers for chain replacement work, with comparable quality when using OEM or upgraded components.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM607 engine prices for your Mercedes Citan

problems.group: Mercedes Citan OM607 EGR Cooler Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Citan OM607 EGR Cooler Failure - The White Smoke & Overheating Fault

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of cracked EGR cooler with revised/updated unit. Coolant flush and refill. EGR valve cleaning if required.
repairOptions.longevity: Permanently resolves the white smoke issue when using updated cooler castings.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine (if head gasket or engine damage occurred)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt OM607 engine including updated EGR cooler and full gasket set.
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary if cooler failure caused overheating and engine warping.

problems.recommendation: The EGR cooler failure is one of the most common Citan diesel faults, but it's rarely a reason to replace the whole engine - unless the owner ignored the symptoms for months and caused overheating damage. Replacing just the EGR cooler at an independent specialist (using a revised post-TSB unit) typically costs under £750 and solves the problem permanently. If you've already seen the temperature gauge climb or lost significant coolant, have a compression test done before deciding on replacement versus repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for OM607 EGR cooler repair and replacement

problems.group: Mercedes Citan M282 Petrol Timing Chain Tensioner Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Citan M282 Petrol Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - The Cold Start Rattle

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full chain kit: timing chain, reinforced hydraulic tensioner (updated part), guides, sprockets, camshaft phasers checked.
repairOptions.longevity: Engine returned to reliable operation with factory-spec timing components.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine (after chain failure)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt M282 engine including updated tensioner, chain kit, full gasket set, and bench testing.
repairOptions.longevity: Required if chain failure caused valve or piston damage.

problems.recommendation: The M282 petrol engine is otherwise reliable, but the tensioner is a known weak point. If you hear chain rattle on cold starts for more than a second or two, don't delay - preventative chain and tensioner replacement at an independent specialist costs around £1,200-£1,800 and will return the engine to full health. This is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement, which becomes necessary once the chain jumps timing. Given that second-generation Citans (W420) are still valuable vehicles, the preventative repair is almost always the right call.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M282 petrol engine prices for your Citan

problems.group: Mercedes Citan OM607 / OM608 DPF & Exhaust Back Pressure Sensor Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Citan OM607 / OM608 DPF & Exhaust Back Pressure Sensor Failure

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Exhaust Back Pressure Sensor Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty exhaust back pressure sensor. Reset DPF adaptions and force regeneration if required.
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves DPF regeneration issues if caught before DPF clogging.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Professional DPF cleaning or replacement of blocked DPF. New pressure sensors fitted.
repairOptions.longevity: Extends DPF life; replacement may be required if cleaning fails.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with new DPF and emissions components.
repairOptions.longevity: Only necessary if engine damage occurred from long-term DPF neglect.

problems.recommendation: The exhaust back pressure sensor is a relatively cheap and quick fix (£150-£250 at an independent) and should be your first port of call if you're experiencing DPF-related warnings or frequent regens. Don't jump straight to DPF cleaning or replacement without having the sensor checked first - many Citan owners have wasted money on DPF work when a £200 sensor was the root cause . If your Citan is used primarily for short urban journeys (which prevents passive DPF regeneration), occasional longer motorway runs can help maintain DPF health.
problems.cta: Get quotes for OM607 / OM608 diesel engine repairs and replacements

problems.h4: Mercedes Citan Diesel Injector Failure - Lean Running, Rough Idle & Power Loss

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostics to identify faulty injector(s). Replace affected injector, recalibrate via ECU, test running.
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000+ miles. Viable when 1-2 injectors have failed.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all 4 injectors. Full ECU calibration, fuel system flush, filter replacement.
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if multiple injectors failing or unknown service history.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with new injectors and fuel system components.
repairOptions.longevity: Only if injector failure caused bore wash or piston ring damage.

problems.recommendation: Single injector failure is common on higher-mileage OM607 engines and doesn't warrant engine replacement. However, before replacing injectors, have a specialist check simpler causes first - a clogged fuel filter can produce identical lean running symptoms and costs under £100 to replace . If you're experiencing rough running and you don't know when the fuel filter was last changed, start there. For injector replacement, independent specialists using OEM or refurbished injectors with calibration included offer significantly better value than main dealers.
problems.cta: Compare OM607 diesel injector replacement quotes for your Citan

problems.group: Mercedes Citan Engine Oil Degradation & Sensor Fault (P253F)
problems.h4: Mercedes Citan Engine Oil Degradation & Sensor Fault (P253F) - Why the ECU Thinks Your Oil Has Failed

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Oil & Filter Change with Proper Specification
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full oil and filter change using Mercedes-spec oil (MB 229.52 or equivalent). Reset service indicator.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable first step; may clear code if incorrect oil was used.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty oil quality/level sensor (located in sump). Oil change and ECU reset.
repairOptions.longevity: Required if P253F persists after correct oil change.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Dealer-level diagnostic equipment (XENTRY) required to recalibrate PCM and adaption values.
repairOptions.longevity: Only available at main dealers or specialists with Mercedes diagnostic tools.

problems.recommendation: Start with the cheapest fix: ensure you're using the correct Mercedes-spec engine oil - poor quality oil or the wrong viscosity can legitimately trigger P253F. If the code reappears within 24 hours of a correct-spec oil change, the oil quality sensor is the likely culprit . An independent Mercedes specialist with XENTRY diagnostic capability can replace the sensor and recalibrate for significantly less than a main dealer. Full engine replacement is not required for this fault unless other damage has occurred.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Citan diesel engine diagnostics and repairs

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes Benz Citan
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with OM607 timing chain rattle, M282 tensioner failure, EGR cooler cracks, DPF sensor problems, or injector issues, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mercedes Benz Citan specialists. Every rebuilt engine supplied through our network includes upgraded components addressing the original failure points, bench testing, and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - with nationwide delivery and supply & fit options available. Don't let a main dealer quote write off your Citan; compare specialist prices and get back on the road without the premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes Benz Citan Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mercedes Benz Citan and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mercedes Benz Citan Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mercedes Benz Citan is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years, power outputs and common failure notes for each fuel type.

groups.title: Mercedes Benz Citan Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz Citan 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5-litre Renault-sourced diesel (K9K platform) formed the backbone of the first-generation W415 Citan (2012-2021) and continued into the second-generation W420 (2021-present). Initially available as the OM607 in three power outputs (75hp, 90hp, 110hp), it was updated to the Euro 6d-compliant OM608 from 2019 onward, featuring AdBlue SCR technology and increased injection pressure for improved efficiency and lower emissions . This engine family powered the vast majority of UK Citan vans and passenger variants, making it the most common replacement query for the model.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Citan 1.5L diesel engines

groups.title: Mercedes Benz Citan Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz Citan 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2-litre turbocharged petrol engine was introduced with the first-generation W415 Citan as the petrol alternative to the diesel range, badged as the 112. This Renault-sourced Energy TCe 115 unit featured direct injection, a stop/start system, and iron-carbon cylinder wall liners for reduced friction. It produced 114hp and was available exclusively with a six-speed manual transmission . A 2015 update added a seven-speed DCT dual-clutch automatic option for certain markets .
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz Citan 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz Citan 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The second-generation W420 Citan (2021-present) introduced a new 1.3-litre turbocharged four-cylinder petrol engine, co-developed within the Renault-Nissan-Mercedes partnership. This unit was available in two power outputs: 102hp (110) and 131hp (113), with the more powerful version also offered with a seven-speed DCT dual-clutch automatic transmission. The engine features direct injection and a gasoline particulate filter (GPF) to meet Euro 6d emissions standards .
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz Citan 1.3L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Mercedes Benz Citan Electric / BEV (Zero Displacement)

items.title: Mercedes Benz Citan eCitan Electric Drive Unit
items.description: Launched alongside the second-generation W420 Citan in 2021, the eCitan is the all-electric variant powered by a 45kWh battery and an electric drive motor. Sharing its underpinnings with the Renault Kangoo E-Tech Electric, the eCitan produces 90kW (122hp) and is offered in both Panel Van and Tourer body styles. It is designed primarily for urban delivery and passenger transport where zero-emission operation is required. For electric vehicles, replacement refers to the drive motor or battery module rather than a combustion engine . Not sure which engine size your Mercedes Benz Citan needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Mercedes Benz Citan specialists.

items.title: Mercedes Benz Citan Diesel Engines
items.description: The diesel range dominates UK Citan replacement enquiries, with the Renault-sourced 1.5-litre OM607 and OM608 families powering over 85% of all Citans sold between 2012 and 2026.
items.descriptor: The diesel range dominates UK Citan replacement enquiries, with the Renault-sourced 1.5-litre OM607 and OM608 families powering over 85% of all Citans sold between 2012 and 2026.
families: OM607.951 (K9K608) - 1.5L 75-110hp, W415 backbone, timing chain stretch common
OM607.951 LA (K9K628) - 1.5L high-output 110hp, 111 CDI and Traveliner
OM608.915 (K9K) - 1.5L Euro 6d, W420 generation, AdBlue system added
foundIn: W420 Citan (2021-2026) - OM608.915 in 110 CDI and 112 CDI
knownFor: Excellent fuel economy (55-65 mpg real-world on motorway runs)
Timing chain stretch affecting high-mileage OM607 units from 60k miles
EGR cooler cracking (documented Mercedes TSB fault)
High replacement demand on fleet 109 CDI and 111 CDI variants

items.title: Mercedes Benz Citan Petrol Engines

items.descriptor: Petrol Citans are significantly rarer than diesel in the UK, limited to the 1.2-litre M200 in the first-generation W415 and the 1.3-litre M282 in the second-generation W420.

M282 timing chain tensioner wear on cold start
Coil pack failure on M200 units
Petrol particulate filter (GPF) on M282 requires occasional regeneration

engineYears.h2: Mercedes Benz Citan Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mercedes Benz Citan production timeline from 2012 to the present, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The first-generation Citan (W415) launched as a rebadged Renault Kangoo, bringing French-sourced petrol and diesel engines to Mercedes commercial customers for the first time .
keyChanges: Launch of W415 generation based on Renault Kangoo II platform
Initially available only in 108 CDI (75hp) and 109 CDI (90hp) diesel
Early cars had five-speed manual gearbox on lower-powered diesels
Petrol variant (112) added from mid-2013 with six-speed manual
knownFor: Ultra-frugal 1.5 dCi diesel delivering 60+ mpg
Early cars restricted to just two engine choices
Renault-sourced engines marketed as "Mercedes-tuned" for better throttle response
Commercial-focused specification with minimal creature comforts
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2013 Mercedes Benz Citan replacement engines
years.ticker: Mercedes Benz Citan 109 CDI engine replacement • OM607.951 timing chain failure • Citan 1.5 diesel engine price
enquiries: Mercedes Benz Citan 109 CDI engine replacement

years.description: Mercedes expanded the Citan range with a higher-output diesel and a petrol option, making the model accessible to a wider audience of commercial and passenger users .
keyChanges: 111 CDI (110hp) diesel added to range from summer 2013
112 petrol (114hp) introduced - 1.2-litre M200 unit
Six-speed manual gearbox on higher-output engines
Three body lengths offered: Compact (3.94m), Standard (4.32m), Long (4.71m)
knownFor: 111 CDI became the most desirable diesel with 110hp and 240Nm torque
Petrol variant offered lower running costs for urban delivery
Timing chain stretch begins affecting higher-mileage OM607 units from 60k miles
Euro V and early Euro VI diesel compliance across the range
years.cta: Compare 2013-2016 Mercedes Benz Citan engine replacement prices
years.ticker: Mercedes Citan 111 CDI engine replacement • OM607 K9K engine price • Citan 1.5 diesel timing chain cost

years.description: Mercedes introduced BlueEFFICIENCY updates to the Citan range, improving fuel economy and reducing emissions across all diesel variants .
keyChanges: BlueEFFICIENCY branding added to diesel variants
CO2 emissions reduced to 112g/km on 109 CDI models
Stop/start system became standard across range
Improved aerodynamics and low-rolling-resistance tyres
knownFor: BlueEFFICIENCY variants deliver 65.7mpg combined
Same OM607 engines - timing chain and EGR cooler issues persist
109 CDI remains the UK fleet favourite
AdBlue system not yet introduced on W415 - Euro VI achieved via NOx trap
years.cta: Get quotes for 2016-2018 Mercedes Benz Citan replacement engines
years.ticker: Citan 109 CDI BlueEFFICIENCY engine • OM607 EGR cooler replacement cost • Mercedes Citan diesel engine for sale
enquiries: Mercedes Citan diesel engine for sale

years.preview: W415 Final Years: Euro 6d-Temp & OM608 Introduction
years.description: From 2018, Mercedes began transitioning the Citan to the updated OM608 diesel engine with AdBlue SCR technology to meet tightening Euro 6d-Temp emissions standards .
keyChanges: Introduction of OM608.915 diesel engine with AdBlue SCR system
New power outputs: 80hp (108 CDI), 95hp (109 CDI), 116hp (111 CDI)
Euro 6d-Temp emissions compliance across diesel range
Six-speed manual gearbox now standard on all diesel variants
mainEngines: M200 - 1.2L petrol (112 - 114hp) - discontinued in some markets
knownFor: AdBlue system added - new failure point on higher-mileage examples
OM608 retains timing chain and EGR cooler vulnerabilities
95hp 109 CDI became best-selling variant of late W415 period
Final years of first-generation Citan production

years.description: The all-new second-generation Citan (W420) arrived in late 2021, based on the third-generation Renault Kangoo with the CMF-CD platform and a revised engine lineup .
keyChanges: New W420 generation on Renault-Nissan CMF-CD platform
Revised OM608 diesel with improved emissions compliance
New M282 1.3-litre turbo petrol engine available (131hp)
Seven-speed DCT dual-clutch automatic transmission option
knownFor: Modernised interior and improved safety - Euro NCAP 5-star on T-Class
New 1.3-litre M282 petrol suffers cam phaser rattle and HPFP issues
Diesel retains AdBlue system - crystallisation issues reported
Three body lengths continue: Compact, Standard, Long

90kW (122hp) electric motor driving front wheels
Available in Panel Van and Tourer body styles
Petrol M282 and diesel OM608 continue unchanged

faq.h2: Mercedes Benz Citan Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mercedes Benz Citan engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt OM607 diesel engine for a Mercedes Benz Citan typically costs £1,200-£3,200 supply only, with supply & fit adding £300-£600. Prices vary by engine code, variant and condition type.
keyPoints: Rebuilt OM607.951 (109 CDI/111 CDI): £1,300-£3,200 supply only
Used Citan engines: £800-£2,000 depending on mileage and warranty
Reconditioned OM608 (W420 models): £2,000-£4,000 supply only
Fitting through our network adds £300-£600 including warranty
Main dealer replacement can cost £1,500-£2,000 more than specialist
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz Citan engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is the Mercedes Citan OM607 diesel engine reliable? What problems should I watch for?
items.answer: The OM607 1.5-litre diesel is generally durable but has three well-documented weak points: timing chain stretch from 60k miles, EGR cooler cracking, and turbo actuator failure. These affect most Citan diesels eventually.
keyPoints: Timing chain rattle on cold start affects many 109 CDI and 111 CDI units
EGR cooler cracks (OEM A6070940001) - documented Mercedes TSB fault
Turbo actuator drift causes loss of boost and reduced power
Proper 5W-30 oil changes every 12k miles significantly extends chain life
Post-TSB EGR coolers and upgraded tensioners resolve these issues
items.cta: Get quotes for a rebuilt OM607 Citan engine with upgraded components

items.question: Does the Citan M282 petrol engine have timing chain problems?
items.answer: Yes. The M282 1.3-litre turbo petrol (fitted to 113 Citan models) suffers from premature timing chain tensioner wear, causing cold start rattle and potential chain failure. Mercedes issued Workshop Bulletin WI-2021-045 addressing this fault.
keyPoints: Tensioner (OEM A2820500201) loses hydraulic pressure from 40k miles
Cold start rattle lasting more than 1-2 seconds indicates tensioner wear
Replacement with reinforced tensioner resolves the issue permanently
Cam phaser rattle is another known M282 weakness
Ignoring symptoms risks chain jump and valve damage
items.cta: Find reconditioned M282 petrol engines for your Citan

items.question: How long does a Mercedes Citan engine replacement take?
items.answer: A straightforward Citan engine swap typically takes 1-2 working days through our specialist network. Mobile fitting may add 1 day for logistics, while complex swaps (petrol to diesel conversions) take longer.
keyPoints: Standard supply & fit: 1-2 days at a fixed workshop location
Mobile engine installation: 1-3 days depending on your location
W415 (2012-2021) swaps are faster due to common parts availability
W420 (2021-present) may require additional ECU programming time
Express service available on high-demand OM607 units through some specialists
items.cta: Book Mercedes Citan supply & fit through our nationwide garage network

items.question: What warranty comes with a reconditioned Mercedes Citan engine?
items.answer: Every reconditioned or rebuilt Citan engine supplied through our network includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour for mechanical failure under normal use.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage - no mileage cap on warranty period
Covers internal engine components, timing system and oil seals
Labour for warranted failures included when fitted by our specialists
M282 tensioner wear excluded unless upgraded part fitted
EGR cooler failure covered only if replaced with revised casting
items.cta: Compare warranty terms on Mercedes Citan rebuilt engines

items.question: Can I fit a used Mercedes Citan engine myself?
items.answer: Yes, but it's not recommended unless you have diagnostic equipment and workshop facilities. Citan engines require ECU coding, AdBlue system calibration (diesel) and timing alignment that most DIY mechanics can't perform.
keyPoints: Engine swaps require lifting equipment and engine support bar
Immobiliser and ECU coding needs Mercedes XENTRY or equivalent
AdBlue system (OM608) requires professional reset after replacement
Injector coding needed on OM607 diesel units
Most suppliers void warranty on DIY installations
items.cta: Get fitted prices for supply & fit Citan engines - professional installation included

items.question: Will a reconditioned Citan engine pass an MOT?
items.answer: Yes. All reconditioned engines supplied through our network are built to meet UK emissions standards. OM608 units (2018+) are Euro 6d-Temp compliant, while earlier OM607 engines meet Euro 5 or Euro 6 standards depending on year.
keyPoints: All rebuilt engines include new DPF where required for diesel variants
EGR coolers replaced with updated crack-resistant castings
Emissions tested before dispatch from specialist rebuilders
M282 petrol engines include GPF (gasoline particulate filter) compliance
Request MOT compliance certificate from your chosen supplier
items.cta: Get a compliant Mercedes Citan engine quote with emissions guarantee

items.question: How do I know if my Mercedes Citan needs a full engine replacement?
items.answer: You need a full replacement if you have timing chain snap, piston ring failure, major overheating damage, or catastrophic injector failure causing bore wash. Minor issues like EGR cooler failure or single injector faults usually just need component replacement.
keyPoints: Timing chain snap always requires full replacement on interference engines
Oil consumption over 1 litre per 600 miles suggests piston ring wear
Overheating leading to warped head or block - replacement cheaper than repair
Multiple injector failure (3-4 injectors) - replacement often better value
Compression test below 250psi across any cylinder indicates serious wear
items.cta: Diagnose your Citan engine fault - compare replacement vs repair costs

items.question: What's the most common engine failure in the Mercedes Citan?
items.answer: EGR cooler cracking on OM607 diesel engines is the most common failure, followed closely by timing chain stretch. The EGR cooler issue is so widespread Mercedes issued a TSB and revised cooler casting.
keyPoints: EGR cooler cracks (A6070940001) cause white smoke and coolant loss
Timing chain stretch affects high-mileage OM607 units from 60k miles
Turbo actuator failure causes loss of power and limp mode
AdBlue crystallisation affects OM608 engines (W420 models)
M282 tensioner wear is most common petrol issue
items.cta: Compare prices for fixing common Citan engine problems

items.question: Are Mercedes Citan engines expensive to replace compared to other vans?
items.answer: Citan engine replacement costs are moderate for its class. A rebuilt OM607 costs £1,200-£3,200, similar to a Ford Transit Connect but less than a VW Caddy. The Renault-sourced engine means parts are widely available and cheaper than pure-Mercedes units.
keyPoints: Citan OM607 rebuild: £1,200-£3,200 - comparable to Kangoo (same engine)
VW Caddy 1.6 TDI: typically £200-£500 more expensive than Citan
Ford Transit Connect 1.5 Ecoblue: similar £1,400-£3,000 range
Parts availability is excellent due to shared K9K platform
Specialist rebuilders are plentiful - unlike rare Mercedes engines
items.cta: Compare Citan engine prices against other compact vans instantly
